historicalFacts: The first official international cricket match was played in 1844 between the United States and Canada.
The first official Test match was played in 1877 between England and Australia in Melbourne, which Australia won.
The first Cricket World Cup was held in England in 1975, and was won by the West Indies.
Sir Don Bradman of Australia holds the highest individual Test batting average of 99.94.
Sachin Tendulkar of India is the highest run-scorer in international cricket history and the only player to score 100 international centuries.
Muttiah Muralitharan of Sri Lanka holds the record for the most wickets in Test (800) and ODI (534) cricket.
A standard cricket pitch is exactly 22 yards (20.12 meters) in length.
The 'Ashes' series between England and Australia was established in 1882 after a mock obituary for English cricket was published.
rules: A standard team consists of 11 players.
An umpire can dismiss a batsman in 10 different ways, including bowled, caught, LBW (Leg Before Wicket), run out, stumped, and hit wicket.
A match is played using a leather ball and a wooden bat, on a field with a central pitch containing wickets at each end.

historicalFacts: The first FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930, and the hosts won the tournament.
Brazil holds the record for the most FIFA World Cup titles, winning it five times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002).
Pelé is the only player to win three FIFA World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970).
The modern rules of association football were first codified in England in 1863 with the founding of the Football Association.
Real Madrid won the first five editions of the European Cup (now UEFA Champions League) from 1956 to 1960.
Lionel Messi holds the record for the most Ballon d'Or awards (8) and the most goals scored in a calendar year (91 goals in 2012).
rules: A match consists of two 45-minute halves, making 90 minutes in total.
Each team has 11 players, including one goalkeeper.
An offside offence occurs if a player is closer to the opponent's goal line than both the ball and the second-last opponent when the ball is played to them.
Yellow cards represent cautions, while a red card results in immediate ejection from the field.

historicalFacts: Basketball was invented in December 1891 by Dr. James Naismith, a Canadian physical education instructor, in Springfield, Massachusetts.
The National Basketball Association (NBA) was founded in 1946 as the Basketball Association of America (BAA).
The Boston Celtics and Los Angeles Lakers are tied for the most NBA championships, with 17 titles each.
Wilt Chamberlain scored an NBA-record 100 points in a single game on March 2, 1962.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls, winning Finals MVP in all six.
LeBron James is the NBA's all-time leading scorer, surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's long-standing record of 38,387 points in 2023.
rules: A standard team has 5 players on the court.
A basket made from within the three-point arc is worth 2 points, while a basket made from beyond the arc is worth 3 points. Free throws are worth 1 point.
Dribbling the ball with both hands simultaneously or stopping and starting dribbling again constitutes a 'double dribble' violation.

historicalFacts: The Wimbledon Championships, founded in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world.
The four Grand Slam tournaments are Wimbledon, the US Open, the French Open, and the Australian Open.
Roger Federer was the first male player to reach 20 Grand Slam singles titles.
Rafael Nadal is known as the 'King of Clay' and holds a record 14 French Open singles titles.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in tennis history.
Serena Williams holds the Open Era record for the most Grand Slam singles titles with 23.
Steffi Graf is the only player to achieve the 'Golden Slam' by winning all four Grand Slams and an Olympic Gold medal in a single calendar year (1888).
rules: Tennis scoring follows: Love (0), 15, 30, 40, Game. If tied at 40-40, it is called 'Deuce', requiring a two-point margin to win.
A standard tennis court measures 78 feet (23.77 meters) long and 27 feet (8.23 meters) wide for singles matches.
Players serve diagonally into the opponent's service box, and are allowed two serves per point.

historicalFacts: Badminton developed in British India in the mid-19th century from an earlier game called battledore and shuttlecock.
The International Badminton Federation (now Badminton World Federation, BWF) was founded in 1934.
Badminton made its debut as an official Olympic sport at the 1992 Barcelona Games.
China has historically dominated badminton, winning the most Olympic gold medals and Uber/Thomas Cup titles.
Lin Dan of China is widely considered the greatest singles badminton player, winning two Olympic golds and five World Championships.
Lee Chong Wei of Malaysia held the World No. 1 ranking for a consecutive 199 weeks between 2008 and 2012.
rules: A match is played with a shuttlecock (or birdie) and lightweight rackets.
The first player/pair to reach 21 points wins a game. If the score is 20-20, a side must win by 2 points.
A shuttlecock landing on a boundary line is considered in-bounds.
The serve must be hit underhand from below the server's waist level.

historicalFacts: Field hockey's modern rules developed in England in the mid-19th century, with the Blackheath Football and Hockey Club being the oldest club (1861).
India is the most successful nation in Olympic field hockey history, winning 8 gold medals, including 6 consecutive golds from 1928 to 1956.
The legendary Dhyan Chand of India is regarded as the greatest field hockey player ever, known for his extraordinary ball control.
Ice hockey originated in Canada in the early 19th century, with the first indoor game played in Montreal in 1875.
The Montreal Canadiens have won the NHL's Stanley Cup a record 24 times.
Wayne Gretzky, known as 'The Great One', holds almost all major NHL scoring records, including most career goals (894) and assists (1,963).
rules: In field hockey, a match consists of four 15-minute quarters, played by 11 players per team.
In ice hockey, a match consists of three 20-minute periods, played by 6 players per team on the ice.
In field hockey, goals can only be scored from inside the 'shooting circle' or 'D'.
The curved side of the hockey stick is considered 'flat' on only one side; players must only use this flat side to hit the ball.

historicalFacts: The first Formula 1 World Championship race was held at Silverstone, England, on May 13, 1950, and was won by Giuseppe Farina.
Michael Schumacher and Lewis Hamilton are tied for the record of most World Drivers' Championships, with seven titles each.
Ferrari is the oldest and most successful active team in Formula 1 history, holding the record for the most Constructors' Championships (16).
Ayrton Senna of Brazil won three World Championships and is legendary for his sensational qualifying speed and rain-driving mastery.
Max Verstappen holds the record for the most wins in a single season (19 wins out of 22 races in 2023).
Sebastian Vettel became the youngest ever World Champion in 2010 at the age of 23 years and 134 days.
rules: A race must not exceed 2 hours in duration, or 3 hours in case of a suspension, and has a minimum distance of 305 km.
Drivers must use at least two different dry tyre compounds during a dry race.
Points are awarded to the top 10 finishers, with 25 points for 1st place and 1 point for 10th place, plus 1 point for the fastest lap (if finishing in the top 10).

historicalFacts: Volleyball was invented in 1895 by William G. Morgan, a YMCA physical education director, in Holyoke, Massachusetts, originally called 'Mintonette'.
The Federation Internationale de Volleyball (FIVB) was founded in Paris in 1947.
Volleyball was added to the summer Olympic program in 1964 in Tokyo.
Karch Kiraly of the USA is the only person to win Olympic gold medals in both indoor volleyball (1984, 1988) and beach volleyball (1996).
The Soviet Union (men) and Cuba (women) have historically been powerhouse teams, with Cuba winning three consecutive Olympic golds in the 1990s.
rules: A team consists of 6 players on the court.
A team is allowed a maximum of three touches of the ball before sending it over the net.
A single player may not touch the ball twice in succession (except when blocking).
Games are played to 25 points (using rally scoring), and must be won by a two-point margin. A deciding fifth set is played to 15 points.
